---
title: "Libraries"
---

Any 3rd party libraries not included in the lists below should be approved before adding them to a Shiny application.
This is to ensure that it is distributed via the proper channels (e.g. PyPI) and is well maintained by the developer.

Try to minimize the libraries used in each app to the minimum needed to perform its intended purpose.
This is to minimize the overhead of maintaining multiple package versions to avoid conflicts.
Additionally, it reduces the amount of packages a user has to install or update if the app is shared locally. 

See below for commonly used libraries:

### Front-End
[shiny](https://shiny.posit.co/py/)\
[shinyswatch](https://posit-dev.github.io/py-shinyswatch/)\
[plotly](https://plotly.com/python/)\

### Back-End
[pandas](https://pandas.pydata.org/)\
[pyodbc](https://github.com/mkleehammer/pyodbc)\
[dataretrieval](https://doi-usgs.github.io/dataretrieval-python/)\
